2605.10186.title: LegalCiteBench: Evaluating Citation Reliability in Legal Language Models
2605.10186.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into legal drafting and research workflows, where incorrect citations or fabricated precedents can cause serious professional harm. Existing legal benchmarks largely emphasize statutory reasoning, contract understanding, or general legal question answering, but they do not directly study a central common-law failure mode: when asked to provide case authorities without external grounding, models may return plausible-looking but incorrect citations or cases. We introduce LegalCiteBench, a benchmark for studying closed-book citation recovery, citation verification, and case matching in legal language models. LegalCiteBench contains approximately 24K evaluation instances constructed from 1,000 real U.S. judicial opinions from the Case Law Access Project. The benchmark covers five citation-centric tasks: citation retrieval, citation completion, citation error detection, case matching, and case verification and correction. Across 21 LLMs, exact citation recovery remains highly challenging in this closed-book setting: even the strongest models score below 7/100 on citation retrieval and completion. Within the evaluated models, scale and legal-domain pretraining provide limited gains and do not resolve this difficulty. Models also frequently provide concrete but incorrect or low-overlap authorities under our evaluation protocol, with Misleading Answer Rates (MAR) exceeding 94% for 20 of 21 evaluated models on retrieval-heavy tasks. A prompt-only abstention experiment shows that explicit uncertainty instructions reduce some confident fabrication but do not improve citation correctness. LegalCiteBench is intended as a diagnostic framework for studying authority generation failures, verification behavior, and abstention when external grounding is absent, incomplete, or bypassed.
2605.10186.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地融入法律草擬和研究工作流程中，錯誤的引用或虛構的先例可能造成嚴重的專業損害。現有的法律基準主要強調法規推理、合同理解或一般法律問題回答，但它們並未直接研究一種中央的普通法失敗模式：當被要求提供案例權威而沒有外部依據時，模型可能返回看似合理但實際上不正確的引用或案例。我們介紹了LegalCiteBench，一個用於研究閉卷引用恢復、引用驗證和案例匹配的法律語言模型基準。LegalCiteBench包含約24,000個評估實例，這些實例是從Case Law Access Project的1,000個真實美國司法意見中構建的。該基準涵蓋五個以引用為中心的任務：引用檢索、引用補全、引用錯誤檢測、案例匹配以及案例驗證和修正。在21個LLMs中，精確的引用恢復在這種閉卷環境中仍然非常具有挑戰性：即使是最強的模型，在引用檢索和補全上得分也低於7/100。在被評估的模型中，規模和法律領域的預訓練提供的增益有限，並未解決這一困難。根據我們的評估協議，模型也經常提供具體但不正確或重疊度低的權威，對於21個被評估模型中有20個在重檢索任務上的誤導性答案率（MAR）超過94%。僅提示的自我放棄實驗顯示，明確的不確定性指示減少了一些自信的虛構，但並未改善引用的正確性。LegalCiteBench旨在作為一個診斷框架，用於研究權威生成失敗、驗證行為以及在缺乏、缺失或繞過外部依據時的自我放棄。
2605.10186.author: Sijia Chen et.al.
2605.10186.authors: Sijia Chen, Hang Yin, Shunfan Zhou
